mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing output, raising corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO level or momentum is below 0.025 over any 3-year window, or negative returns occur in >55% of top-quartile INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate indicates labor market weakening, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If in triggered instances (UNRATE rise >0.3pp in 3mo) the S&P 500 produces positive returns in >60% of cases or average return is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation is elevated and the federal funds rate is high, the monetary squeeze compresses valuations and raises recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If in months satisfying both conditions the S&P 500 median forward return is positive or Rank IC of the interaction term is <0.02 over 2 years, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During the expansion phase following a recession, rapid GDP growth signals strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:U.S. economy entered expansion after 2020 recession
failure_condition: If over any post-recession period with GDP growth >2% the S&P 500 forward 60-day return is lower than the unconditional median or negative in >50% of quarters, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals weakening construction and consumer sentiment, reducing economic momentum and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
regime_hint: housing downturn or rising rates environment
failure_condition: If in months with HOUST decline >5% the S&P 500 5-day return is positive in >50% of cases or average return is >0, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer spending, which bolsters corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth on forward 20-day returns below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling period, or positive returns in less than 60% of months with above-median RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment and industrial production confirm synchronized labor and output expansion, boosting investor confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and INDPRO rise month-over-month
failure_condition: If when both series rise MoM the S&P 500 5-day return is negative in >50% of cases or average return is not positive, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, each month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate contracts liquidity and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve raised rates seven times in 2022-2023
failure_condition: In months with FEDFUNDS increase, if the S&P 500 forward 20-day return is positive in >40% of cases or average return >0 over the tightening cycle, reject.
